operating_state: Total net sales increased 54% to $89.6 billion driven by higher sales across all product categories and services. iPhone net sales increased 66% from successful new model launches and favorable mix, while Mac and iPad posted strong double-digit growth. Services revenue increased 27% driven by App Store, advertising and cloud services growth. All geographic regions delivered strong double-digit growth with particularly robust performance in Greater China and Rest of Asia Pacific.
cost_structure: Total gross margin percentage increased to 42.5% from 38.4% driven by higher leverage, different product mix and foreign currency strength. Products gross margin percentage improved to 36.1% from 30.3% due to higher volume and mix benefits. Services gross margin percentage increased to 70.1% from 65.4% driven by different services mix and higher leverage. Operating expenses declined as percentage of sales to 12% from 16% as revenue growth outpaced expense increases.
material_events: The company repurchased $19.0 billion of common stock and paid $3.4 billion in dividends during the quarter. In April 2021, Apple announced new products including M1-powered iMac and iPad Pro, Apple TV 4K with redesigned Siri Remote, AirTag accessory, and Apple Podcasts Subscriptions. The company issued $13.9 billion in new debt and repaid $4.5 billion during the six-month period.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continued to affect business operations with retail stores and channel partner locations temporarily closed at various times, and most employees working remotely. The company has begun reopening offices and retail stores subject to operating restrictions and local regulations. Foreign currency movements provided favorable impact on net sales across multiple regions.
forward_outlook: On April 28, 2021, the Board of Directors increased share repurchase authorization by $90 billion and raised quarterly dividend from $0.205 to $0.22 per share beginning in Q3 2021. Management believes existing cash balances and liquidity arrangements will satisfy working capital needs, capital purchases, dividends, share repurchases and debt obligations over the next 12 months.
uncertainty_profile: The full extent of future COVID-19 impact remains uncertain and depends on factors outside company control including pandemic duration, vaccine distribution effectiveness, and global economic impact. The company maintains deemed repatriation tax payable under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act to be paid in installments, while €14.1 billion related to the State Aid Decision remains in escrow pending conclusion of legal proceedings.
